title: Jeremias Ivan
text: A Bioinformatician who is passionate about Evolutionary Biology, including genomics, phylogenetics, and methods development.

title: Methods
text:
PhyloNOW (Phylogenomic Non-Overlapping Windows) is an R pipeline to partition chromosome alignments into non-overlapping windows with variable sizes based on local AIC improvements. It consists of two main steps: iterative splitting and iterative merging of neighbouring windows, which has been shown to outperform fixed-size approach.

 GitHub
 Publication

title: Methods
text:
PhyloNOW (Phylogenomic Non-Overlapping Windows) is an R pipeline to partition chromosome alignments into non-overlapping windows with variable sizes based on local AIC improvements. It consists of two main steps: iterative splitting and iterative merging of neighbouring windows, which has been shown to outperform fixed-size approach.

 GitHub
 Publication

title: Methods
text:
SimNOW
SimNOW (Simulation Non-Overlapping Windows) is an R pipeline to assess the accuracy of non-overlapping windows approach in simulated phylogenetic studies. It starts with sequence simulation, followed by non-overlapping window analyses using fixed window sizes and/or variable window sizes.

 GitHub
 Publication

title: Methods
text:
StepwiseNOW
StepwiseNOW (Stepwise Non-Overlapping Windows) is an R pipeline to partition chromosome alignments into non-overlapping windows with fixed sizes. It consists of iterative pairwise comparison between two window sizes at a time, including only windows that can be analysed using both sizes. This approach has been superseded by PhyloNOW.

 GitHub
 Publication
